title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds authorized inclusive site-audit charter from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of authorized inclusive site-audit charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features.
Record who controls each target, permitted identities, paths, methods, times, data uses, and revocation contact before any automated request.
Separate RFC 9309 crawl preferences from contracts, copyright, privacy, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Decision
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 7120 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate useful owner-authorized, privacy-minimized, inclusive crawl outcomes and claim layers; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Decision.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Stakeholder Decision, keep Stakeholder Decision connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: Downloading many pages is itself a valuable stakeholder outcome. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Stakeholder Decision.

For commit to the mission model, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Stakeholder Decision for authorized inclusive site-audit charter.
State the Stakeholder Decision preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
